message:
fix(build): populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep layout (ft-ibtjf, gh-52)

Default workspace layout declares `mux` and `frankenterm-term` as path
dependencies, so Cargo.lock has no `source = "git+...#hash"` fragment to
extract from. `select_vendored_metadata` returned (None, None, None),
`option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolved to None, and
`vendored.rs:155` unconditionally reported `[ERR] WezTerm vendored -
vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine build.

Adds a path-dep fallback branch that runs `git -C <vendored-root>
rev-parse HEAD` when the lockfile has no git source. For non-git
workspaces (cargo-install tarball, shipped source archive), falls
back to a deterministic `local-path-build-<hash>` sentinel derived
from the mux/term manifests — the prefix is non-hex so
`commit_matches()` can never false-match a real wezterm SHA, and the
digest changes whenever the vendored surface drifts.

Also:
- rename the term lookup key from `wezterm-term` (stale) to
  `frankenterm-term` so the term package actually resolves in
  Cargo.lock lookups
- emit `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` for both vendored manifests so
  metadata refreshes on vendored bumps
- preserve `(None, None, None)` when the vendored tree is genuinely
  absent so `vendored.rs` fail-closed guard still fires
- ship `#[cfg(test)]` harness in build.rs covering all four cases:
  git+hash source, path-dep in git checkout, path-dep in non-git
  tree, missing-both; verified via
  `rustc --test build.rs && ./build_test` (8 passed)

Direct build.rs execution now emits:
  cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=<40-char hex SHA>
  cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION=0.1.0
  cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE=path+<vendored-root>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
